question_title: What will be the value of "Overnight Repurchase Agreements: Treasury Securities Purchased by the Federal Reserve in the Temporary Open Market Operations" on 2026-03-26?
summary: The RPONTSYD series (overnight repo operations) is extremely zero-inflated: about 50% of days report exactly 0.0, and over 83% of mid-month days are ≤ 0.01 billion. March 26, 2026 is a regular Thursday with no special characteristics — not month-end, quarter-end, holiday-adjacent, or near an FOMC meeting (which was March 17-18). The exact same date in 2025 was 0.0. The recent Jan-Mar 2026 regime has returned to near-zero baseline after the elevated Oct-Dec 2025 period. The distribution is heavily concentrated at 0 with a long right tail for unlikely stress scenarios.

factors.description: Overwhelming base rate of 0 or near-0: ~83% of mid-month days (4th-27th) have values ≤ 0.01, 49.6% are exactly 0.0
March 26, 2025 (exact same date last year) was exactly 0.0; entire last week of Q1 March 2025 was near-zero
Current regime (Jan-Mar 2026) has returned to low activity after Oct-Dec 2025 stress period; 81.6% of 2026 days ≤ 0.01
March 26 is a regular Thursday with no special characteristics: not month-end, not quarter-end, not holiday-adjacent, not near FOMC (which was March 17-18)
Monthly ~0.1 small value exercises clustered around 8th-13th of month; March 26 is well past this window
Tail risk: unexpected liquidity stress or quarter-end approach demand could trigger repo activity; Oct 2025 showed this can emerge suddenly

reasoning:
**Forecast Summary:**

| Percentile | Value (Billions USD) |
|---|---|
| 10th | 0.000 |
| 20th | 0.000 |
| 40th | 0.000 |
| 60th | 0.001 |
| 80th | 0.010 |
| 90th | 0.100 |

**Key reasoning:**
- This series is almost always 0 or near-0 on regular business days. About half of all days report exactly 0.0, and 83%+ of mid-month days are ≤ $0.01B.
- March 26, 2026 (Thursday) has no special characteristics — it's not month-end, quarter-end, holiday-adjacent, or near an FOMC meeting.
- The exact same date in 2025 was 0.0, and the entire last week of Q1 2025 was near-zero.
- The Jan-Mar 2026 regime has returned to baseline after the unusual Oct-Dec 2025 liquidity stress period.
- The small ~$0.1B operations that appear regularly are clustered around the 8th-13th of each month (likely small value exercises), well before the 26th.
- Tail risk exists from unexpected liquidity stress, but there are no current indicators of such stress.

resolution_criteria: Resolves to the value found on the FRED API for the series RPONTSYD once the data is published.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will paginate through the data found at the API endpoint `https://api.stlouisfed.org/fred/series/observations?series_id=RPONTSYD`. This endpoint includes the latest data for the series. The latest revised data will be used when the resolution is assessed. The datapoint matching 2026-03-26 will be used to determine the resolution of this question.

A datapoint matches if:
1. The series is updated daily and the date of the datapoint is within 1 day previous to the resolution date.
2. The series is updated weekly and the date of the datapoint is within 7 days previous to the resolution date.
3. The series is updated monthly and the date of the datapoint is within 31 days previous to the resolution date.

If the datapoint is clearly skipped, or no datapoint is found after 2 weeks of checking then the question will be annulled.
